Package recoder.kit

Class TypeKit

java.lang.Object
recoder.kit.TypeKit
public class TypeKit extends Object
this class implements basic functions for type handling.

  • Method Details

    • createTypeReference

      public static TypeReference createTypeReference(ConstructorDeclaration decl)
      Factory method that creates a new type reference fitting to the constructor declaration. The reference is not prefixed as its use context is unknown. This method is useful for creating factory classes.
      Parameters:
      decl - a constructor declaration.
      Returns:
      the newly created type reference, parent links are valid.

    • createTypeReference

      public static TypeReference createTypeReference(ProgramFactory f, String qualifiedName)
      Factory method that creates a type reference with prefices (UncollatedReferenceQualifiers) from a qualified name.
      Parameters:
      f - the program factory to use.
      qualifiedName - a qualified (= potentially dotted) name.
      Returns:
      a type reference to the given type; parent links of the reference are made valid.

    • makeTypeArgRef

      public static ASTList<TypeArgumentDeclaration> makeTypeArgRef(ProgramFactory f, List<? extends TypeArgument> tas)

    • createTypeReference

      public static TypeReference createTypeReference(ProgramFactory f, Type t, boolean addTypeArgs)

    • createTypeReference

      public static TypeReference createTypeReference(ProgramFactory f, Type t)
      Factory method that creates a new type reference derived by the name of the given type.
      Parameters:
      f - the program factory to be used.
      t - the type which shall be referenced.
      Returns:
      a type reference to the given type; parent links of the reference are made valid.

    • createTypeReference

      public static TypeReference createTypeReference(SourceInfo si, Type t, ProgramElement context)
      Factory method that creates a new type reference derived by the name of the given type and tries to minimize its qualification using the given context for the type reference. The context should describe the position the type reference will be inserted into, usually a statement container.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info to be used.
      t - the type which shall be referenced.
      context - a program element from which the type shall be addressed (may not be null).
      Returns:
      a minimal type reference to the given type; parent links of the reference are made valid.

    • createAbstractSuperClass

      public static InterfaceDeclaration createAbstractSuperClass(NameInfo ni, ClassDeclaration cdecl, String abstractsupername) throws NameClashException
      creates an abstract super class (interface) for the given class.
      Parameters:
      ni - the NameInfo to ensure that a class of the sme name as the new superclass does not yet exist
      cdecl - public class to abstractify
      abstractsupername - the name of the new abstract superclass
      Throws:
      NameClashException

    • createInterfaceDeclaration

      public static InterfaceDeclaration createInterfaceDeclaration(ClassDeclaration decl)
      Create an interface from a class declaration. This is done by omission of all elements that cannot be transformed. The following members are kept:
      • Public FieldDeclarations that are final and static and are initialized (in a list of specifications, only the variables that have initializers are kept),
      • public MethodDeclarations that are no ConstructorDeclarations, and not static,
      • public InterfaceDeclarations and ClassDeclarations (weird, but admissible).
      The interface does not extend any of the interfaces implemented by the class declaration by default. The name of the returned declaration corresponds to "Abstract" + decl.getName(), which can be changed afterwards.

    • createAdapterClass

      @Deprecated public static ClassDeclaration createAdapterClass(String adapterName, ClassDeclaration classDecl)
      Deprecated.
      needs rework
      Create a simple adapter class for a class declaration. If the class is

      class c {

      m(int i, int i2) { ..}

      m2(int i, int i2) { ..}

      }

      the created class is

      class cAdapter {

      m(int i, int i2) { delegatingObject.m(i,i2); }

      m2(int i, int i2) { delegatingObject.m2(i,i2); }

      }

      Parameters:
      adapterName - the String specifying the name of the adapter class
      classDecl - the ClassDeclaration of the class to be adapted
      Returns:
      the ClassDeclaration of the generated adapter class

    • rename

      @Deprecated public static boolean rename(ChangeHistory ch, CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, NameInfo ni, TypeDeclaration type, String newName)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by recoder.kit.transformation.RenameType
      Transformation that renames a type declaration and all known references to that type. The new name should not hide another type in the declaration context.
      Parameters:
      ch - the change history (may be null).
      xr - the cross referencer service.
      ni - the name info service.
      type - the type declaration to be renamed; may not be null .
      newName - the new name for the element; may not be null and must denote a valid identifier name.
      Returns:
      true, if a rename has been necessary, false otherwise.

    • getInfluencedReferences

      @Deprecated public static List<TypeReference> getInfluencedReferences(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, String newTypeName, NonTerminalProgramElement context)
      Deprecated.
      still untested.
      retrieves the influenced type reference
      Parameters:
      xr - the CrossReferenceSourceInfo to find usages
      newTypeName - the String with the new type name
      context - the NonTerminalProgramElement defining the scope
      Returns:
      the list of type referenced influenced by the renaming

    • getReferences

      public static List<TypeReference> getReferences(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, Type t, NonTerminalProgramElement root, boolean scanTree)
      Query that retrieves all references to a given type that are contained within the given tree. The specified flag defines the strategy to use: either the cross reference information is filtered, or the cross reference information is collected from the tree. The filtering mode is faster if the tree contains more nodes than there are global references to the given type.
      Parameters:
      xr - the cross referencer to use.
      t - a type.
      root - the root of an arbitrary syntax tree.
      scanTree - flag indicating the search strategy; if true, local cross reference information is build, otherwise the global cross reference information is filtered.
      Returns:
      the list of references to the given type in the given tree, can be empty but not null.

    • getMembers

      public static List<Member> getMembers(ClassTypeContainer ctc)
      Query that collects all members of a class type, a method, or a package. For a package, this includes all class types of that package, for a class type, this includes all defined constructors, fields, methods, and inner types, and for a method, this includes all inner types.
      Parameters:
      ctc - the class type to collect members from.
      Returns:
      a mutable list of all members of the given class type.

    • getSuperClass

      public static ClassType getSuperClass(NameInfo ni, ClassType ct)
      Query returing the super class of the given class type. If the class type is an interface or has no explicit extended type, java.lang.Object will be reported (also for java.lang.Object itself).
      Parameters:
      ni - the name info service to use.
      ct - the class type to get the super class from.
      Returns:
      the super class.

    • isLessVisible

      public static boolean isLessVisible(Member x, Member y)
      Query comparing the visibility of two members.
      Parameters:
      x - the first member.
      y - the second member.
      Returns:
      true if the first member is less visible than the second (in the order "private" - "" (package) - "protected" - "public" where applicable), false if it is as least as visible.

    • isCovered

      public static boolean isCovered(ProgramModelInfo pmi, List<? extends ClassType> x, List<? extends ClassType> y)
      Checks if for each class type in the first type list there is a super type in the second. This is useful to check if a exception lists is less or equally strict that the other.
      Parameters:
      pmi - the type system info to use.
      x - a class list, may not be null.
      y - a class list, may not be null.
      Returns:
      true if the first list of class types is covered by the second one, false otherwise.

    • isValidInterfaceMember

      public static boolean isValidInterfaceMember(MemberDeclaration member)
      Query that checks if the given member is admissible within an interface. Admissible are public final static completely-initialized fields, public non-static non-constructor body-less methods, and public interface and class declarations. This method does not criticize the use of redundant modifiers such as abstract or static.
      Parameters:
      member - a potential interface member.
      Returns:
      true if the given member could become a member of an interface, false otherwise.

    • getCoveredSubtypes

      public static List<? extends ClassType> getCoveredSubtypes(ProgramModelInfo pmi, List<? extends ClassType> list)
      Gets all types in the given list that are subtypes of other types in the list.
      Parameters:
      pmi - the program model info service to use.
      list - a list of class types.
      Returns:
      a list of class types that have supertypes in the list.
      Since:
      0.71
      See Also:
      • removeCoveredSubtypes(recoder.service.ProgramModelInfo, java.util.List<recoder.abstraction.ClassType>)

    • removeCoveredSubtypes

      public static List<ClassType> removeCoveredSubtypes(ProgramModelInfo pmi, List<ClassType> list)
      Removes types in the given list that are subtypes of other types in the list and returns the removed types in a new list.
      Parameters:
      pmi - the program model info service to use.
      list - a mutable list of class types.
      Returns:
      a set of class types that have supertypes in the list and that have been removed.
      Since:
      0.71
      See Also:
      • getCoveredSubtypes(recoder.service.ProgramModelInfo, java.util.List<? extends recoder.abstraction.ClassType>)

    • getRedundantSuperInterfaces

      public static List<TypeReference> getRedundantSuperInterfaces(SourceInfo si, TypeDeclaration td)
      Query that returns all redundant super interfaces of the specified type declaration. This does not cover redundant extensions of java.lang.Object, but does cover double extensions or implementations of interfaces as well as transitive interface inheritance.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info service to use.
      td - the type declaration to check for inheritance redundancy.
      Since:
      0.71

    • getRedundantExceptions

      public static List<TypeReference> getRedundantExceptions(SourceInfo si, Throws t)
      Query that returns all redundant exceptions of the specified throws clause. This does not cover redundant extensions of java.lang.RuntimeException or subclasses, but does cover double declarations as well as transitive exceptions.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info service to use.
      t - the throws clause to check for redundancy.
      Since:
      0.71
